spec_paraphrase: MoE forward not restated; spec assumes top-k MoE structure (e.g. Qwen3 N=128, top-k=8).
notes: Background equation; spec doesn't need to restate. Not a finding.

spec_paraphrase: Frequency saliency not used; spec uses REAP saliency (Eq. 9 of 2510.13999) per §5 Step 1.
notes: Background baseline; correctly omitted (paper ablation shows REAP saliency is mandatory, ΔAVG=-8.7 if replaced by freq).

spec_paraphrase: S_j = (1/|X_j|) Σ g_j(x)·||f_j(x)||₂ over active tokens — Stage 2 §5 Step 1 cites paper 2510.13999 Eq. 9, formula identical to REAM Eq. 3.
notes: Spec credits 2510.13999 Eq. 9 (REAP source) for the formula REAM also calls Eq. 3 — equivalent.

spec_paraphrase: Naive output similarity not used; spec uses gated similarity (Eq. 8) per §5 Step 2 δ̃_expert.
notes: Background baseline correctly skipped — paper ablation shows σ-scaling is essential (ΔGEN=-11.5).

spec_paraphrase: δ_gate(i,j) = cosine similarity between pre-softmax router logit profile vectors per expert across calibration tokens.
notes: Spec correctly uses pre-softmax logits (paper says raw g(x), not σ); matches Eq. 5.

spec_paraphrase: W_merged = Σ (freq_i/Σfreq_j)·P_i(W_i) — frequency-weighted average with Hungarian permutation P_i.
paper_verbatim: W_merged = (sum S^freq_i W_i)/(sum S^freq_i), where W_i are weight matrices with neuron permutation alignment (Ainsworth et al., 2023) applied w.r.t. centroid
notes: Spec (line 259) and ch12 D5b (line 736) characterize Eq. 6 as 'no permutation', BUT paper Eq. 6 explicitly states 'with neuron permutation alignment (Ainsworth et al., 2023)'. The implementation (Hungarian + C_act+C_wt) actually matches the paper's REAM extension §4 (claim #18, Hungarian on C<ci,j>=C_act+C_wt). So implementation is paper-faithful, but D5b's 'paper says' is misdescribed. Finding emitted: paraphrase_drift on D5b paper-says column.

spec_paraphrase: δ_REAM(i,j) = δ_gate + δ̃_expert (unweighted sum, equal weight 1.0).
notes: Identical.

spec_paraphrase: δ̃_expert uses gated outputs σ(x)_e·E_e(x); denominator |X| because gates suppress inactive tokens — explicitly noted to match paper Eq. 8.
paper_verbatim: tilde-delta_E(i,j) = (1/|X|) sum sim(sigma(x)_i E_i(x), sigma(x)_j E_j(x))
notes: Spec is precise about |X| denominator and σ-scaling — matches Eq. 8 exactly.

spec_paraphrase: Step 3 Greedy Pseudo-Pruning: top-N'_l REAP centroids, iterate centroids in descending saliency, each absorbs most similar non-centroid up to max_merge_group_size.
paper_verbatim: designate the N' experts with the highest saliency as cluster centroids ... sort them in decreasing order of saliency. Then, starting from c_1, we greedily assign to it up to C unassigned non-centroid experts E_j most similar to c_1 based on delta_REAM
notes: Algorithm faithful.

spec_paraphrase: Spec doesn't explicitly note singleton-pass-through behavior, but the greedy assignment step naturally produces it.
paper_verbatim: most centroids receive no assignments and form singleton groups that pass through unchanged
notes: Implicit consequence of the algorithm; not a spec gap.

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses max_merge_group_size=8 with budget-bump fallback; paper uses C=16 for Qwen3-30B at 25% reduction.
paper_verbatim: fixed to 16 for Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507 (when compressed to 96 experts)
notes: D5a sanctions the deviation as conservative (8 < 16). Justification: 'caps any single centroid's absorption at half the paper's cap, keeping merge groups smaller and more homogeneous; the budget bump compensates'. Self-contained reasoning (not h200/resume), so sanction_justification=ch12. No empirical ablation cited — informational only.

spec_paraphrase: Spec targets 25% reduction (Qwen3-30B 128→96) per §5 default; 50% regime not addressed.
notes: Spec doesn't target 50% reduction; correctly omitted.

spec_paraphrase: Spec targets Qwen3-30B family (256-expert variant), not 80B Coder/Next.
notes: Out of scope for spec model.

spec_paraphrase: Spec targets Qwen3 family.
notes: Out of scope.

spec_paraphrase: Spec mentions '1024 calibration samples' for Stage 2 covariance accumulation (line 274). Paper uses 3072 sequences × 512 tokens.
notes: Calibration size discrepancy: spec=1024 vs paper=3072 sequences. No Ch.12 row covers this. May materially affect saliency/similarity quality. Finding emitted.

spec_paraphrase: Spec doesn't enumerate Table 3 mixing ratios — these are for paper's mixture sweep analysis.
notes: Sweep is a paper experiment, not a method requirement.

spec_paraphrase: Spec does not specify calibration mixture ratio (C4:Math:Code).
paper_verbatim: we fix the mixture at a code heavy ratio of 0:0.3:0.7 to favor the overall GEN score
notes: Spec silent on calibration data composition for Stage 2. Paper-anchored choice (0:0.3:0.7) not reflected. Finding emitted.

spec_paraphrase: C = C_wt + C_act with C_wt = gate+up Frobenius weight distance, C_act = per-neuron mean activation L2 distance.
notes: Implementation matches paper. The 'deviation' label on D5b is a misnomer (the paper itself defines and uses this; see eq6 finding).

spec_paraphrase: Hungarian algorithm on combined cost C = C_wt + C_act aligns child expert intermediate neurons to centroid before averaging.
paper_verbatim: Li et al. (2024) used the Hungarian algorithm with cost matrix C_wt ... we introduce a combined cost matrix C<ci,j> = C_act + C_wt ... optimal permutation reorders weights of expert j
notes: Faithful implementation of REAM §4 alignment.

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses combined C=C_wt+C_act (matches paper main config); ablation result is informational only.
paper_verbatim: activation-only alignment yields the smallest penalty (Delta_AVG = -0.5)
notes: Ablation evidence supports the spec's choice.

spec_paraphrase: Sequential merging implemented with early-exit forward optimization: layers processed in order, each layer's profiling forward executes layers 0..L only with early-exit hook on L+1; activations reflect prior merges. Mathematically identical to paper's sequential merging.
paper_verbatim: after merging layer ell, a second forward pass is run through this layer to recompute its activations to be used by the subsequent layer ell+1
notes: Spec preserves paper semantics exactly while optimizing wall-clock. Note minor framing nuance: paper says 'second forward through merged layer', spec runs forward through 0..L (the prior layers were already merged). Equivalent in result.

spec_paraphrase: Spec doesn't restate paper's wall-time anchor.
notes: Anchors for H200 forecasts can use this — H200 regime concern.

spec_paraphrase: n/a — informational baseline.
paper_verbatim: Removing all our components together would make REAM equivalent to MC-SMoE
notes: Lineage statement; no spec impact.

spec_paraphrase: Step 5 Router Resize: 'Remove merged experts' rows from gate.weight. Update num_experts on the MoE block.'
paper_verbatim: we follow REAP and remove the rows of the gate weights that are not corresponding to centroid experts
notes: REAP-style row removal implemented.

spec_paraphrase: Spec resizes num_experts but does not change top-k (only gate rows removed; routing config preserved).
notes: Top-k preserved by construction; spec doesn't touch top-k config.

spec_paraphrase: Spec adds Stage 2.5 (post-merge router KD) — train mlp.gate.weight after merging. Paper evaluates 'without any fine-tuning after compression'.
paper_verbatim: We follow evaluation in REAP ... and evaluate all methods without any fine-tuning after compression
notes: Spec extends REAM with router KD (Stage 2.5/Stage 5 from paper 2603.02217). Strictly speaking this adds post-compression training, departing from REAM's static evaluation protocol. Not in Ch.12 (because Stage 2.5 is justified by paper 2603.02217, not REAM). Borderline finding — spec freely combines two papers' methods. Recording as info.

spec_paraphrase: Stage 2 reduces routed experts from 256 to ~180-200 (about 22% reduction), close to paper's 25% regime.
paper_verbatim: compress models by merging 25% or 50% of the experts per layer
notes: Spec at ~22% reduction, paper's 25% regime. Ratio reasonable. Note: spec's '256 experts' suggests Qwen3-Next/80B, not Qwen3-30B (which has 128). See claim_topk_unchanged context.

spec_paraphrase: Spec cites '98.4% of original quality on Qwen3-30B at ~22% reduction' (line 206).
notes: Spec quotes a slightly different statistic (98.4% retention); paper Table 1 GEN ratio = 69.8/70.9 = 98.4%. Consistent.

notes: Not referenced.

notes: Spec choice supported by ablation.

notes: Critical: spec explicitly preserves σ-scaling per paper Eq. 8.

spec_paraphrase: All four REAM components implemented: (1) δ_REAM similarity (Step 2), (2) pseudo-pruning (Step 3), (3) C_act+C_wt cost (Step 4 alignment), (4) sequential merging (line 210).
notes: Full REAM implementation.

spec_paraphrase: Spec: 'Iterate centroids in descending saliency order'.
paper_verbatim: sort them in decreasing order of saliency
notes: Order preserved.

spec_paraphrase: max_merge_group_size caps absorption per centroid; spec uses 8 (paper uses C=16 for Qwen3-30B at 25%).
paper_verbatim: we greedily assign to it up to C unassigned non-centroid experts E_j
notes: Same deviation as hp_C_qwen3_30b_96 — D5a sanctioned.

spec_paraphrase: Per-layer centroid set: 'For each layer L (processed in order)... Top-N'_l experts by REAP score become centroids'.
paper_verbatim: for each layer ell, we designate the N' experts with the highest saliency as cluster centroids C_ell
notes: Per-layer scoping matches paper.

spec_paraphrase: Spec targets Qwen3 (256-expert variant, likely Qwen3-Next-80B per N=256, top-k context).
notes: Note spec mentions '256 to ~180-200 experts' which is between Qwen3-30B (128) and Qwen3-Next (512). Possible inconsistency with paper-anchored model identity. Worth flagging.

spec_paraphrase: Stage 2.5 trains the gate weight after merging — departs from REAM's pure-static protocol.
paper_verbatim: Our work is along the static direction ... no additional training
notes: Same as claim_no_finetune — Stage 2.5 router KD is justified by a different paper (2603.02217), but blends with REAM's static frame. Not addressed in Ch.12. Recorded once already; pointer.

spec_paraphrase: Spec describes C_act as 'per-neuron mean activation L2 distance from ReamCostAccumulator.get_neuron_mean'. Paper says vectors bar-H are 'normalized'.
notes: Spec uses 'mean' (per-neuron mean) — paper says 'normalized'. Implementation detail unclear from spec text whether normalization happens. Possible silent omission of the normalization step. Finding emitted.

spec_paraphrase: Implicit consequence of weighted-merge formula when group size is 1.
notes: Numerical equivalence implied by the formula; not a separate spec claim required.

spec_paraphrase: Spec Step 4 uses 'frequency-weighted merge' (W_merged = Σ (freq_i/Σfreq_j)·P_i(W_i)) — i.e., S^freq weights, NOT REAP saliency S^reap weights.
paper_verbatim: REAM merges with REAP saliency-weighted average (REAP saliency listed as a core component; Eq. 6's S^freq is the MC-SMoE baseline form generalized to REAP saliency in REAM)
notes: Ambiguous whether spec's 'freq_i' means routing frequency (S^freq, Eq. 2) or REAP saliency (S^reap, Eq. 3). Step 1 computes REAP scores; Step 4 says 'freq_i'. If literal frequency, this contradicts REAM (which uses REAP-weighted merge — paper's lineage MC-SMoE→REAM swaps S^freq for S^reap). Finding emitted: spec may use frequency weights instead of REAP saliency for the merge averaging coefficients. Needs implementation check.

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses 1024 calibration samples (per line 274) vs. paper's 3072 sequences × 512 tokens.
